One-Shot NAS proposes to train the supernet using the path dropout technique and then conduct the architecture search by inheriting the pre-trained supernet weights to sub-models for fast evaluation.

Gradient-based approaches relax the architectural parameters to be continuous and jointly optimize the architectural parameters and supernet weights using gradient descent.

The CIFAR-10 dataset is used to search for superior cells in the DARTS search space.
The DARTS search space contains superior cells, and the supernet is composed of normal and reduction cells within this space.
Normal cells process the feature map without downsampling, which indicates a direct relation between them.
Reduction cells perform down-sampling on the feature map, demonstrating a close relationship between them.
Reduction cells perform down-sampling on the feature map with stride = 2, indicating a specific operation performed by them.
Reduction cells are located at the 1/3 and 2/3 of the total depth of the supernet, showing a positional relation between the two terms.
Each cell consists of seven nodes with four intermediate nodes, suggesting a composition relation between cells and nodes.
Each cell consists of fourteen edges with eight candidate operations on each edge, indicating the presence of multiple operations in a cell.
We search for the most two powerful operations for each edge to get the final searched cell, highlighting the selection process of operations for edges.
Our method achieves the highest average test accuracy 97.52 ± 0.07, surpassing the original DARTS and its advanced variants.

edge: When compared with other improved one-shot NAS methods such as NSAS, Few-Shot-NAS, GM, and CLOSE, our method consistently outperforms them with the least search cost.
Our best cells are shown in Fig.3.
We can observe that both the normal cell and the reduction cell have the skip connect operation from the input nodes, leading to a residual link with other operations.
Such a ResNet-style residual link is helpful for achieving state-of-the-art performance, demonstrating that our method excels in identifying excellent architectures.
